private channels, while `private_exclude` will exclude them.

sort.description:
Possible values are:
- `relevant`: search ranking based on what we think is closest,
- `name`: alphabetical,
- `member_count`: number of users in the channel,
- `created`: date channel was created.
Defaults to `member_count`.
You can optionally pair this with the `sort_dir` argument to change how it is sorted.

total_count_only.description:
Only return the total count of channels.
Omits channel data and allows access for admins without channel manager permissions. Defaults to `false`.

sort_dir.description: Change sort direction to ascending (`asc`) or descending (`desc`). Defaults to `desc`.

_metadata.description: A group of settings that describe the manifest.

display_information.description:
A group of settings that describe parts of an app's appearance within Slack. If you're distributing
the app via the Slack Marketplace, read our [listing guidelines](https://docs.slack.dev/slack-marketplace/distributing-your-app-in-the-slack-marketplace) to pick the best values for these settings.

features.description: A group of settings corresponding to the Features section of the app config pages.

oauth_config.description: A group of settings describing OAuth configuration for the app.

settings.description: A group of settings corresponding to the Settings section of the app config pages.

functions.description: A group of settings corresponding to Custom Functions bundled in your application.

major_version.description:
An integer that specifies the major version of the manifest schema to target.
Only `1` and `2` are supported. Defaults to `1`.

minor_version.description: An integer that specifies the minor version of the manifest schema to target.

name.description: A string of the name of the app. Maximum length is 35 characters.

description.description: A string with a short description of the app for display to users. Maximum length is 140 characters.

long_description.description: A string with a longer version of the description of the app. Maximum length is 4000 characters.

background_color.description:
A string containing a hex color value (including the hex sign) that specifies the background color
used on hovercards that display information about your app. Can be 3-digit (`#000`) or 6-digit (`#000000`) hex
values. Once an app has set a background color value, it cannot be removed, only updated.

app_home.description:
A subgroup of settings that describe [App Home](https://docs.slack.dev/surfaces/app-home) configuration.
[App Home](https://docs.slack.dev/surfaces/app-home).

bot_user.description:
A subgroup of settings that describe [bot user](https://docs.slack.dev/legacy/legacy-bot-users) configuration.
[Legacy bots](https://docs.slack.dev/legacy/legacy-bot-users).

shortcuts.description:
An array of settings groups that describe [shortcuts](https://docs.slack.dev/interactivity/implementing-shortcuts)
configuration. A maximum of 10 shortcuts can be included in this array.
[Shortcuts](https://docs.slack.dev/interactivity/implementing-shortcuts).

slash_commands.description:
An array of settings groups that describe [slash commands](https://docs.slack.dev/interactivity/implementing-slash-commands)
configuration. A maximum of 50 slash commands can be included in this array.
[Slash Commands](https://docs.slack.dev/interactivity/implementing-slash-commands).

unfurl_domains.description:
An array of strings containing valid unfurl domains to register. A maximum of 5 unfurl domains can be
included in this array.
[Link unfurling: configuring domains](https://docs.slack.dev/messaging/unfurling-links-in-messages).

home_tab_enabled.description:
A boolean that specifies whether or not the [Home tab](https://docs.slack.dev/surfaces/app-home#home-tab) is enabled.
[Home tab](https://docs.slack.dev/surfaces/app-home#home-tab).

messages_tab_enabled.description:
A boolean that specifies whether or not the Messages tab in your App Home is enabled.
[Message tab](https://docs.slack.dev/surfaces/app-home#messages-tab).

messages_tab_read_only_enabled.description:
A boolean that specifies whether or not the users can send messages to your app in the
[Messages tab](https://docs.slack.dev/surfaces/app-home#messages-tab)
of your App Home.
[Messages tab](https://docs.slack.dev/surfaces/app-home#messages-tab).

display_name.description:
A string containing the display name of the bot user. Maximum length is 80 characters.
Allowed characters: `a-z`, `0-9`, `-`, `_`, and `.`.

always_online.description: A boolean that specifies whether or not the bot user will always appear to be online.

type.description: Specifies which [type of shortcut](https://docs.slack.dev/interactivity/implementing-shortcuts) is being described.

name.description: A string containing the name of the shortcut.

callback_id.description: A string containing the callback_id of this shortcut. Maximum length is 255 characters.

description.description: A string containing a short description of this shortcut. Maximum length is 150 characters.

command.description:
A string containing the actual slash command. Maximum length is 32 characters, and should include
the leading `/` character.

description.description:
A string containing a description of the slash command that will be displayed to users.
Maximum length is 2000 characters.

should_escape.description:
A boolean that specifies whether or not channels, users, and links typed with
the slash command should be escaped. Defaults to `false`.

url.description:
A string containing the full `https` URL that acts as the
[slash command's request URL](https://docs.slack.dev/interactivity/implementing-slash-commands#creating_commands).

usage_hint.description: A string a short usage hint about the slash command for users. Maximum length is 1000 characters.

redirect_urls.description:
An array of strings containing [OAuth redirect URLs](https://docs.slack.dev/authentication/installing-with-oauth).
A maximum of 1000 redirect URLs can be included in this array.

scopes.description: A subgroup of settings that describe [permission scopes](https://docs.slack.dev/reference/scopes) configuration.

bot.description:
An array of strings containing [granular bot scopes](https://docs.slack.dev/reference/scopes?token_types=Bot)
to request upon app installation. A maximum of 255 scopes can included in this array.

user.description:
An array of strings containing [user scopes](https://docs.slack.dev/reference/scopes?token_types=User)
to request upon app installation. A maximum of 255 scopes can included in this array.

allowed_ip_address_ranges.description:
An array of strings that contain IP addresses that conform to the
[Allowed IP Ranges](https://docs.slack.dev/authentication/best-practices-for-security) feature. Maximum of 50 IP addresses.

event_subscriptions.description: A subgroup of settings that describe [Events API](https://docs.slack.dev/apis/web-api/using-the-conversations-api) configuration for the app.

incoming_webhooks.description: A subgroup of settings that describe [Incoming Webhooks](https://docs.slack.dev/messaging/sending-messages-using-incoming-webhooks) configuration for the app.

interactivity.description: A subgroup of settings that describe [https://docs.slack.dev/interactivityinteractivity](https://docs.slack.dev/interactivityinteractivity) configuration for the app.

token_rotation_enabled.description: A boolean that specifies whether or not [token rotation](https://docs.slack.dev/authentication/using-token-rotation) is enabled.

A string acting as a unique identifier for a block. If not specified, a `block_id` will be generated.
You can use this `block_id` when you receive an interaction payload to
[identify the source of the action](https://docs.slack.dev/interactivity/handling-user-interaction#payloads).
Maximum length for this field is 255 characters. `block_id` should be unique for each message and each iteration of
a message. If a message is updated, use a new `block_id`.

text.description:
A [PlainTextElement](PlainTextElement) that defines the button's text. `text` may truncate with ~30 characters.
Maximum length for the text in this field is 75 characters.

value.description:
The value to send along with the [interaction payload](https://docs.slack.dev/interactivity/handling-user-interaction#payloads).
Maximum length for this field is 2000 characters.

url.description:
A URL to load in the user's browser when the button is clicked. Maximum length for this field is 3000
characters. If you're using `url`, you'll still receive an [interaction payload](https://docs.slack.dev/interactivity/handling-user-interaction#payloads)
and will need to send an [acknowledgement response](https://docs.slack.dev/interactivity/handling-user-interaction#acknowledgment_response).

style.description:
Decorates buttons with alternative visual color schemes. Use this option with restraint.
`primary` gives buttons a green outline and text, ideal for affirmation or confirmation actions. `primary` should
only be used for one button within a set.
`danger` gives buttons a red outline and text, and should be used when the action is destructive. Use `danger` even
more sparingly than primary.
If you don't include this field, the default button style will be used.

accessibility_label.description:

readers instead of the button `text` object. Maximum length for this field is 75 characters.

among all other `action_id`s in the containing block. Maximum length for this field is 255 characters.

timestamp.description: A UNIX timestamp for the date to be displayed in seconds.

format.description:
A template string containing curly-brace-enclosed tokens to substitute your provided `timestamp`
in a particularly-formatted way. For example: `Posted at {date_long}`. The available date formatting tokens are:
- `{day_divider_pretty}`: Shows `today`, `yesterday` or `tomorrow` if applicable. Otherwise, if the date is in
  current year, uses the `{date_long}` format without the year. Otherwise, falls back to using the `{date_long}`
  format.
- `{date_num}`: Shows date as YYYY-MM-DD.
- `{date_slash}`: Shows date as DD/MM/YYYY (subject to locale preferences).
- `{date_long}`: Shows date as a long-form sentence including day-of-week, e.g. `Monday, December 23rd, 2013`.
- `{date_long_full}`: Shows date as a long-form sentence without day-of-week, e.g. `August 9, 2020`.
- `{date_long_pretty}`: Shows `yesterday`, `today` or `tomorrow`, otherwise uses the `{date_long}` format.
- `{date}`: Same as `{date_long_full}` but without the year.
- `{date_pretty}`: Shows `today`, `yesterday` or `tomorrow` if applicable, otherwise uses the `{date}` format.
- `{date_short}`: Shows date using short month names without day-of-week, e.g. `Aug 9, 2020`.
- `{date_short_pretty}`: Shows `today`, `yesterday` or `tomorrow` if applicable, otherwise uses the `{date_short}`
  format.
- `{time}`: Depending on user preferences, shows just the time-of-day portion of the timestamp using either 12 or
  24 hour clock formats, e.g. `2:34 PM` or `14:34`.
- `{time_secs}`: Depending on user preferences, shows just the time-of-day portion of the timestamp using either 12
  or 24 hour clock formats, including seconds, e.g. `2:34:56 PM` or `14:34:56`.
- `{ago}`: A human-readable period of time, e.g. `3 minutes ago`, `4 hours ago`, `2 days ago`.
TODO: test/document `{member_local_time}`, `{status_expiration}` and `{calendar_header}`

[`block_actions` payload](https://docs.slack.dev/reference/interaction-payloads/block_actions-payload) for. Should be

The thumbnail's longest dimension will be scaled down to 75px while maintaining the aspect ratio of the image.
The file size of the image must also be less than 500 KB.
For best results, please use images that are already 75px by 75px.

bottom-right of the view. `submit` is required when an input block is within the `blocks` array. Max length of 24

clear_on_close.description:

clicks the close button. Defaults to `false`.

Maximum length of 3000 characters.
